rulings.Case Title: LORENZO LOTTA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 15, 2008, line 2, DEFENDANT DIAGEO NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BASED ON FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED EVIDENCE THAT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE MOORE DECLARATION DOES NOT DO SO FOR THERE IS NO FOUNDATION FOR MOORE'S STATEMENT THAT THE MATERIALS BEING SWEPT UP CONTAINED ASBESTOS. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GIVENS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens Per Ccp Sections 418.10(A), 418.10(A)(2) And 410.30
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 15, 2008, line 5, DEFENDANT ROCKWELL AUTOMATION INC.'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens Per CCP Sections 418.10(A), 418.10(A)(2) And 410.30 IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANT, TO DATE, HAS ONLY SHOWN THAT LESS THAN ONE-THIRD OF DEFENDANTS ARE SUBJECT TO SUIT IN MISSOURI. THE FACT THAT MISSOURI HAS A LONG ARM STATUTE IS NOT SUFFICIENT FOR DEFENDANT TO MEET ITS BURDEN UNDER HANSEN V. OWENS-CORNING 51 CAL. APP. 4TH 753, 757. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: DALE RILEY, SR. VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P): AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 15, 2008, line 7, DEFENDANT HAYWARD ELECTRIC COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S FACTUALLY DEVOID SPECIAL INTERROGATORY RESPONSES AND PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO AYDELOTTE DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED FOR LACK OF FOUNDATIONAL FACTS. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. DEFENDANT'S OJBECTIONS 1, 3, 7-9 ARE SUSTAINED. ONE IS BASED ON D'AMICO, THREE ON RELEVANCE AND 7-9 BASED ON RELIANCE ON INADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: REA RENE LAMPARAS-MCCULLOGH VS. PENSON INC (DBA PENSON TRUST COMPANY) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 15, 2008, line 13, DEFENDANT PENSCO, INC. (DBA PENSCO TRUST COMPANY), MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO PREVAIL ON EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONSTRUCTIVE DISCHARGE AND THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR HARASSMENT. THE DIRECTOR'S CONDUCT WAS SUFFICIENTLY PERVASIVE AND SEVERE TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN A CAUSAL NEXUS BETWEEN HER PROTECTED CONDUCT AND THE ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION, HER LEAVE. DEFENDANT WAS OBLIGATED TO PLACE PLAINTIFF ON LEAVE IN RESPONSE TO HER DOCTOR'S NOTES AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PROVIDED A BASIS THAT SHE WAS ENTITLED TO PAID LEAVE. THE COMMON LAW CAUSES OF ACTION ARE DUPLICATIVE BUT ARE NOT BARRED BY FEHA. WORKER'S COMPENSATION DOES NOT BAR THE NEGLIGENT INFLICTION CLAIM BECAUSE OF THE EXTREME NATURE OF THE DIRECTOR'S CONDUCT. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 AND 7 ARE OVERRULED. THE FOLLOWING EVIDENCE GOES TO PLAINTIFF'S STATE OF MIND AND EXPLAINS HER DELAY IN REPORTING THE ACTS OF HARASSMENT: 2, 3, 4, 5 AND 7. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE EVIDENCE OFFERED IN THE REPLY IS GRANTED. =(302/PJM/PB)
